title: Topic, Headers, Alternate, and Exchange-to-Exchange Routing · Transfer Build
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers topic, headers, alternate, and exchange-to-exchange routing into a new multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.
Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match.
Use headers exchanges only with typed bounded metadata and explicit all-versus-any matching, not as a schema-free routing escape hatch.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Topic Segment Design
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 3237 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Topic Segment Design.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Topic Segment Design, keep Topic Segment Design connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

Competency probe: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review.
